source: Pi session 2026-05-14 — session memory lost when Groq 429 at exit
title: Trigger L1->L2 distillation every N turns mid-session, not just at exit
what_failed: Distillation only ran at clean session exit. If Groq hit rate-limit at exit (T-071) OR the process crashed OR the user just closed the terminal, the entire session was lost to L2.
where_failed: agent/session.py::on_exit was the only call site for distill_session.
why_likely: Original design assumed clean exits were the norm. In practice, rate-limit-at-exit and abrupt closures are common.
suggested_fix: Trigger distillation every 10 turns in a background thread. Track _last_distilled_turn so each batch processes only NEW L1 rows. Exit-time distillation still runs as a final sweep, but no longer load-bearing.
